title: Amylotheca samoensis

uses: Amylotheca samoensis, also known as the Samoan rosewood, has been used in traditional Samoan medicine for various purposes. The plant's bark and leaves are believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. Traditionally, it has been used to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. In addition, the plant has been used as a remedy for gastrointestinal issues and as a treatment for wounds. Further research is needed to confirm the plant's efficacy and safety for medicinal use.

The plant has been traditionally used in medicine, and its health benefits include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which have been attributed to its ability to alleviate fever, wounds, and respiratory issues.
